sn.s: Teníamos planes para ir a la playa el sábado.
sn.e: We had plans to go to the beach on Saturday.

sn.s: Pero el carro de Roberto se dañó.

sn.e: "Relax, we'll find a plan B," I respond.
"What do we do then?" he asks.

sn.s: "Podemos tomar el bus. Llegaremos un poco tarde."
sn.e: "We can take the bus. We'll arrive a little late."

sn.s: "Pero igual tendremos toda la tarde," agrego.
sn.e: "But we'll still have the whole afternoon," I add.

sn.s: "Buena idea. No había pensado en eso."
sn.e: "Good idea. I hadn't thought of that."

sn.s: "Mañana arreglás el carro y todo bien."
sn.e: "Tomorrow you fix the car and all's well."

sn.s: Cuando un plan falla, se busca otro.
sn.e: When one plan fails, you find another.

sn.s: Hablar del futuro: iremos, tendremos, llegaremos.
sn.e: Talking about the future: we'll go, we'll have, we'll arrive.

sn.s: La flexibilidad es parte de vivir aquí.
sn.e: Flexibility is part of living here.

sn.s: Al final, lo importante es estar juntos.
sn.e: In the end, what matters is being together.
